evals.name: cafe-sales-full-arc
evals.prompt: 우리 카페 24개월치 월별 매출 데이터야 (cafe_sales.csv). 요즘 매출이 정체된 것 같은데 왜 그런지 분석해서 분석 노트북이랑 결과 리포트까지 만들어줘.
evals.expected_output: Full workflow: frames/verifies the plateau problem, sets a measurable goal, plans method, builds a Jupyter notebook (one action per cell, markdown interpretation between cells), and writes a self-contained HTML report with a limitations section. Ideally surfaces the reframe that revenue is propped up by promo-driven new customers while repeat customers decline.

assertions.text: Produces a Jupyter notebook (.ipynb) file
Produces a self-contained HTML report file
Notebook interleaves markdown cells that interpret results (not just code)
Report contains an explicit limitations / 보완할 점 section
Output shows a stated, measurable analysis goal before diving into code
Conclusions cite specific numbers from the data
Surfaces that repeat customers are declining despite flat/rising revenue (reframe)
Profiles the data — per-column null-rate, distributions, and an outlier/duplicate check — as a distinct step before modeling
Confirms or prepares a validated modeling table (handles or explicitly clears missing values, outliers, and duplicates) before building the notebook

evals.name: open-data-sourcing-subway
evals.prompt: 서울 지하철 역별·시간대별 승하차 공공데이터로 출퇴근 시간 혼잡도 분석 프로젝트를 하고 싶어. 데이터 찾는 것부터 분석, 리포트까지 진행해줘.
evals.expected_output: Frames the congestion problem with evidence, sets a measurable goal, identifies a real open dataset and investigates its actual access method (portal, API/CSV, schema, encoding), plans method, and outlines the notebook + report. Should probe/verify the data source rather than assume field names.

assertions.text: Identifies a concrete real open dataset and its access method (portal/API/CSV)
Investigates actual schema/fields/access rather than assuming them
States a measurable analysis goal tied to a decision
Plans methods that fit the data characteristics (time-of-day, station granularity)
Plan includes producing a notebook and an HTML report with limitations

evals.name: ambiguous-goal-forces-framing
evals.expected_output: Because the goal is vague, the workflow should not dive straight into generic EDA — it should frame the problem and pin down a concrete, measurable goal (ideally via brainstorming with the user) before substantial analysis, then proceed.

assertions.text: Does NOT immediately dump generic EDA without framing a problem/goal
Explicitly works to define a concrete, measurable goal first
Asks the user or proposes specific candidate questions to narrow scope

evals.name: messy-data-strict-prep
evals.prompt: 이 카페 매출 데이터로 매출 추세 분석해줘 (cafe_sales_messy.csv). 결측치랑 이상해 보이는 값이 좀 있는 것 같아.
evals.expected_output: The data has injected quality issues: missing values (repeat_customers, avg_ticket, promo_spend), outliers (a ~8x revenue spike and a ~10x avg_ticket), a negative customer count (impossible value), and a duplicate month row. The workflow should PROFILE the data (Stage 4) — surfacing the null-rates, outliers, the duplicate, and the impossible negative — then PREPARE & VALIDATE the table (Stage 6) with explicit, reasoned treatment of each issue (impute/drop/cap/keep-as-signal, dedup, fix/flag the negative) plus a logged before/after, before any modeling. Treatment choices should be justified against the data shape and goal, not applied blindly.

assertions.text: Profiles the data and reports per-column null-rate / distributions before modeling
Detects the missing values and handles them with a stated treatment (impute or drop) and a reason, not silently
Detects the outliers (revenue spike, avg_ticket spike) and decides cap / drop / keep-as-signal with justification
Detects and resolves the duplicate month row
Flags the impossible negative customer count as a range/validity violation
Logs the before/after of cleaning (every drop/impute documented) and validates the prepared table before building the notebook
Does not blindly delete all flagged rows or mean-impute without considering the series/seasonality
